entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop (Sorghum bicolor L. moench) used for grain production.

entities.name: STRIGA ASIATICA
entities.description: A parasitic weed (Striga asiatica (L.) Kuntze) that causes yield losses in sorghum.

entities.name: REGRESSION APPROACH
entities.description: A statistical method used to predict grain yield losses based on Striga counts.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, used to measure loss caused by Striga.

entities.name: CSH 1
entities.description: A susceptible hybrid cultivar of sorghum.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the experiments were conducted and yield losses were estimated.

entities.name: CHECKERBOARD TRIALS
entities.description: Experimental design used to obtain grain yield and Striga counts data.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The seasonal period during which average grain yield loss was 17.5%.

entities.name: POST-RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The seasonal period during which average grain yield loss was 25.2%.

entities.name: STRIGA INFESTATION
entities.description: The presence and level of Striga asiatica affecting the crop area.
